dev_plans.content: Lightweight per-epoch probe using raw numpy/pandas inside the validation step. Implementation steps: (1) At the END of each validation pass (no gradient work, purely forward inference on the fixed val split), load the ethnicity one-hot labels (5 groups: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) and the ground-truth death-class label for every val patient/sequence. (2) Apply the FROZEN decision threshold of 0.5 to the model's death-class probability — this constant is hard-coded inside prober.py (e.g., `DEATH_THRESHOLD = 0.5`) and must NOT be exposed as a train.py argparse flag, a hyperparameter, or picked via val-set tuning. (3) For each of the 5 ethnic groups g, compute recall_g = TP_g / (TP_g + FN_g); if a group has zero positives in a batch, record recall_g = NaN and drop it from the gap computation for that epoch only (but log a warning). (4) Compute gap_max_min = max(recall_g) - min(recall_g) and spread_std = std(recall_g, ddof=1). (5) Write per-epoch JSON row: {epoch, recall_per_group (dict), gap_max_min, spread_std, anchor_bce_loss, anchor_auprc}. (6) Persist a CSV of the time series and save a PNG with two lines for gap_max_min (solid) plus horizontal rules at the standard and acceptable thresholds. (7) If gap_max_min >= 0.10 at any epoch AND anchor_auprc has not dropped >10% from epoch-1 value, emit a structured WARNING log line so the orchestrator can surface a parity failure without aborting training. No resampling, no smoothing, no post-hoc correction; this is intentionally the cheapest possible signal that catches a model learning an ethnicity-correlated decision boundary.
dev_plans.metric: max(recall_g) - min(recall_g) across the 5 ethnicity groups, computed at frozen threshold=0.5 on the validation set each epoch (absolute recall gap, lower is better, range [0,1])

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous probe that accompanies the point-estimate gap with a non-parametric bootstrap confidence interval and an explicit hypothesis test, so that noisy epochs cannot trivially pass or fail the probe. Implementation steps: (1) After each epoch's validation forward pass, keep the per-sample (death_label, ethnicity_idx, pred_prob) triples in memory (or on-disk shard) for the full val set. (2) At threshold=0.5 (hard-coded as a module constant, never a train.py arg), compute per-group recall on the realized sample exactly as in Plan 1, but ALSO perform B=2000 stratified bootstrap resamples over patients (not over rows, to respect temporal repeats within a stay): for each bootstrap replicate b, recompute recall_g^(b) for each group and gap^(b) = max - min. (3) From the bootstrap distribution, report: point_estimate = mean(gap^(b)), lower_ci = percentile(gap^(b), 2.5), upper_ci = percentile(gap^(b), 97.5), and a one-sided p-value for H0: P(gap > 0.05) estimated as the fraction of bootstrap replicates with gap^(b) > 0.05. (4) Per-epoch record: {epoch, per_group_recall, point_estimate, lower_ci, upper_ci, p_value_superiority, anchor_bce_loss, anchor_auprc}. (5) PASS criterion uses the upper_ci: the epoch is considered 'parity-safe' only if upper_ci <= 0.05 (i.e., we are 95% confident the true gap is below the standard threshold). This is deliberately stricter than the point-estimate rule in Plan 1 and protects against spurious passes on batches where a minority group has very few positives. (6) Save a CSV time series plus a plot with two bands: the bootstrap CI band around the gap trajectory, and horizontal rules at the standard (0.05) and acceptable (0.10) thresholds. (7) If anchor_auprc degrades >10% from epoch 1 while upper_ci <= 0.05, flag it as 'parity via utility collapse' (the anchor guard should catch this, but flag it explicitly here too).
dev_plans.metric: upper bound of the 95% bootstrap CI (2000 patient-level resamples) on per-epoch max(recall_g) - min(recall_g) at frozen threshold=0.5 on the validation set (lower is better, range [0,1])

dev_plans.content: Tooling-based probe that delegates the arithmetic to fairlearn (group-fairness library) so the implementation inherits audited, well-tested metric classes and can drop into a broader fairness dashboard without custom code. Implementation steps: (1) Add `fairlearn>=0.10` to requirements (and `matplotlib` for its built-in plots). (2) Inside the per-epoch validation hook, extract y_true (death), y_pred (argmax of pred_prob > 0.5), sensitive_features (argmax of the 5-way ethnicity one-hot, mapped back to categorical labels). The threshold 0.5 is set as a constant inside prober.py and is never read from config or CLI. (3) Instantiate `fairlearn.metrics.MetricFrame(metrics={'tpr': sklearn.metrics.recall_score}, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_pred, sensitive_features=sensitive_features, )` — the `tpr` entry is exactly equal-opportunity recall for the death class. (4) Compute `group_tprs = mf.by_group` (Series indexed by ethnicity), `gap_max_min = mf.difference()` — this is fairlearn's canonical max-min difference which by definition equals max(recall_g) - min(recall_g). Also pull `mf.group_min()` and `mf.group_max()` for the dashboard, plus `mf.overall` for cross-check against the anchor AUPRC's recall component. (5) Per-epoch JSON row: {epoch, group_tprs (dict), gap_max_min, group_min, group_max, anchor_bce_loss, anchor_auprc}. (6) Emit a PNG via `fairlearn.metrics.MetricFrame` plotting helpers (bar chart of per-group recall with the overall recall line) for the round report — this is the artifact reviewers will look at. (7) Save the fairlearn CSV export of the MetricFrame for auditability. (8) Apply the same anchor guard: if gap_max_min <= standard_threshold but anchor_auprc drops >10% from epoch-1 value, mark the epoch with a structured warning. Because fairlearn's `difference()` is the canonical equal-opportunity gap, the thresholding rules carry over unchanged: PASS if gap_max_min <= 0.05; acceptable-line is at 0.10 for the soft-stop signal.
dev_plans.metric: fairlearn MetricFrame.difference() applied to recall_score at frozen threshold=0.5 across the 5 ethnicity groups on the validation set per epoch — i.e., max(recall_g) - min(recall_g) (lower is better, range [0,1])
